sentences.sentence: Humans start developing a sense of humour as early as six weeks old, when babies begin to laugh and smile in response to stimuli.
sentences.translation: 人类早在六周大时就开始发展幽默感，那时婴儿会开始对刺激作出大笑和微笑的反应。

structure.text: start developing a sense of humour
as early as six weeks old
when babies begin to laugh and smile in response to stimuli

difficult_points: start developing 表示“开始形成/发展”，developing 后接抽象名词 a sense of humour。
as early as 强调时间之早，说明幽默感的发展比人们可能预想的更早。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明六周大这一时间点上婴儿的具体表现。

sentences.sentence: Laughter is universal across all human cultures and even exists in some form in rats, chimps, and bonobos.
sentences.translation: 笑存在于所有人类文化之中，甚至也以某种形式存在于老鼠、黑猩猩和倭黑猩猩身上。

structure.text: is universal across all human cultures
and even exists in some form in rats, chimps, and bonobos

difficult_points: universal across all human cultures 表示笑是跨文化普遍存在的现象。
and even exists 并列第二个谓语，even 突出这种现象不只限于人类。
in some form 表示形式可能不同，避免理解成动物的笑与人类完全相同。

sentences.sentence: Like other human emotions and expressions, laughter and humour provide psychological scientists with rich resources for studying human psychology, ranging from the development of language to the neuroscience of social perception.
sentences.translation: 和其他人类情绪与表达一样，笑和幽默为心理科学家研究人类心理提供了丰富资源，研究范围从语言的发展到社会感知的神经科学。

structure.text: Like other human emotions and expressions
provide psychological scientists with rich resources
ranging from the development of language to the neuroscience of social perception

difficult_points: provide somebody with something 是固定结构，表示“为某人提供某物”。
for studying human psychology 说明 rich resources 的用途。
ranging from...to... 后置补充研究资源覆盖的范围。

sentences.sentence: Theories focusing on the evolution of laughter point to it as an important adaptation for social communication.
sentences.translation: 关注笑的进化的理论把笑视为一种用于社会交流的重要适应性特征。

structure.text: Theories focusing on the evolution of laughter
focusing on the evolution of laughter
point to it as an important adaptation

difficult_points: focusing on... 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 Theories。
it 指代前文的 laughter，不是泛指情况。
point to...as... 表示“把……指出/解释为……”。

sentences.sentence: Take, for example, the recorded laughter in TV comedy shows.
sentences.translation: 以电视喜剧节目中录制的笑声为例。

structure.text: the recorded laughter in TV comedy shows

difficult_points: Take...for example 是举例时常用的祈使结构。
recorded laughter 指预先录制的笑声，不是现场观众当下的反应。

sentences.sentence: Back in 1950, US sound engineer Charley Douglass hated dealing with the unpredictable laughter of live audiences, so started recording his own 'laugh tracks'.
sentences.translation: 早在1950年，美国音响工程师查利·道格拉斯讨厌处理现场观众不可预测的笑声，于是开始录制自己的“笑声轨道”。

structure.text: hated dealing with the unpredictable laughter of live audiences
so started recording his own 'laugh tracks'
dealing with the unpredictable laughter of live audiences

difficult_points: Back in 1950 用来把时间拉回过去，带有“早在当时”的语气。
hated 后接动名词 dealing with，表示不喜欢处理这件事。
so started 省略了重复主语 Charley Douglass，与前一个谓语构成因果并列。

sentences.sentence: These were intended to help people at home feel like they were in a social situation, such as a crowded theatre.
sentences.translation: 这些笑声轨道旨在帮助家中的观众感觉自己仿佛处在一种社交场景中，比如拥挤的剧院里。

structure.text: were intended to help people at home
feel like they were in a social situation
like they were in a social situation

difficult_points: These 指前一句的 'laugh tracks'，不是泛指这些节目。
were intended to 是被动语态，表示这些录音被设计出来的目的。
feel like 后接从句，表达“感觉好像……”。

sentences.sentence: Douglass even recorded various types of laughter, as well as mixtures of laughter from men, women, and children.
sentences.translation: 道格拉斯甚至录制了各种类型的笑声，以及来自男性、女性和儿童的混合笑声。

structure.text: as well as mixtures of laughter from men, women, and children

difficult_points: even 强调录制内容之细致，不仅有单一笑声类型。
as well as 连接两个宾语成分，后者是 mixtures of laughter。
from men, women, and children 后置修饰 laughter，说明声音来源。

sentences.sentence: In doing so, he picked up on a quality of laughter that is now interesting researchers: a simple 'haha' communicates a remarkable amount of socially relevant information.
sentences.translation: 在这样做的过程中，他注意到了笑声的一种特质，而这种特质如今正引起研究人员的兴趣：一个简单的“哈哈”会传达大量与社会关系相关的信息。

structure.text: picked up on a quality of laughter
a simple 'haha' communicates a remarkable amount of socially relevant information

difficult_points: In doing so 指代前面录制多种笑声这一做法。
pick up on 表示“注意到、察觉到”，不是字面上的捡起。
冒号后的完整分句解释 a quality of laughter 的具体内容。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 a quality of laughter。

expressions.meaning_zh: 与社会关系相关的信息

sentences.sentence: In one study conducted in 2016, samples of laughter from pairs of English-speaking students were recorded at the University of California, Santa Cruz.
sentences.translation: 在2016年进行的一项研究中，加州大学圣克鲁兹分校录下了成对英语学生的笑声样本。

structure.text: samples of laughter from pairs of English-speaking students
at the University of California, Santa Cruz

difficult_points: conducted in 2016 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 study。
samples of laughter 是主语核心，后面的 from... 说明样本来源。
were recorded 是被动语态，突出笑声样本被记录下来。

sentences.sentence: A team made up of more than 30 psychological scientists, anthropologists, and biologists then played these recordings to listeners from 24 diverse societies, from indigenous tribes in New Guinea to city-dwellers in India and Europe.
sentences.translation: 随后，一个由30多名心理科学家、人类学家和生物学家组成的团队，把这些录音播放给来自24个不同社会的听众，从新几内亚的原住民部落到印度和欧洲的城市居民都有。

structure.text: A team made up of more than 30 psychological scientists, anthropologists, and biologists
made up of more than 30 psychological scientists, anthropologists, and biologists
from indigenous tribes in New Guinea to city-dwellers in India and Europe

difficult_points: made up of... 是过去分词短语，说明 team 的组成。
then 标记实验步骤顺序，表示录音之后被播放给听众。
from...to... 说明24个社会的范围，跨度从部落到城市居民。

expressions.meaning_zh: 从原住民部落到城市居民

sentences.sentence: Participants were asked whether they thought the people laughing were friends or strangers.
sentences.translation: 参与者被问及他们认为正在笑的人是朋友还是陌生人。

structure.text: whether they thought the people laughing were friends or strangers

difficult_points: were asked 是被动语态，表示参与者被研究者提问。
whether 引导宾语从句，表示问题内容是二选一判断。
the people laughing 中 laughing 后置修饰 people，意为“正在笑的人”。

sentences.sentence: On average, the results were remarkably consistent: worldwide, people's guesses were correct approximately 60% of the time.
sentences.translation: 平均来看，结果非常一致：在全球范围内，人们的猜测大约有60%的时候是正确的。

structure.text: worldwide, people's guesses were correct approximately 60% of the time

difficult_points: On average 限定统计结果，表示这是平均水平而非每个人都如此。
冒号后的分句解释 remarkably consistent 的具体表现。
60% of the time 表示“在60%的情况下”，不是持续时间的60%。

sentences.sentence: Researchers have also found that different types of laughter serve as codes to complex human social hierarchies.
sentences.translation: 研究人员还发现，不同类型的笑声充当着复杂人类社会等级结构的信号代码。

structure.text: that different types of laughter serve as codes to complex human social hierarchies

difficult_points: have also found 是现在完成时，说明这是研究累积出的发现。
that 引导宾语从句，说明发现的具体内容。
serve as codes 表示笑声“起到信号/代码作用”，不是字面服务。

sentences.sentence: A team led by Christopher Oveis from the University of California, San Diego, found that high-status individuals had different laughs from low-status individuals, and that strangers' judgements of an individual's social status were influenced by the dominant or submissive quality of their laughter.
sentences.translation: 由加州大学圣迭戈分校的克里斯托弗·奥维斯领导的一个团队发现，高地位个体的笑声不同于低地位个体，而且陌生人对一个人社会地位的判断会受到其笑声中支配性或服从性特质的影响。

structure.text: A team led by Christopher Oveis from the University of California, San Diego
led by Christopher Oveis from the University of California, San Diego
that high-status individuals had different laughs from low-status individuals
and that strangers' judgements of an individual's social status were influenced by the dominant or submissive quality of their laughter
were influenced by the dominant or submissive quality of their laughter

difficult_points: led by... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 A team。
found 后并列接两个 that 从句，分别说明笑声差异和陌生人判断受影响。
were influenced by 是被动语态，强调判断受到笑声特质影响。
their laughter 中 their 指 an individual's，而不是 strangers。

sentences.sentence: In their study, 48 male college students were randomly assigned to groups of four, with each group composed of two low-status members, who had just joined their college fraternity group, and two high-status members, older students who had been active in the fraternity for at least two years.
sentences.translation: 在他们的研究中，48名男大学生被随机分成四人一组，每组由两名低地位成员和两名高地位成员组成；低地位成员刚加入大学兄弟会，高地位成员则是已经在兄弟会中活跃了至少两年的高年级学生。

structure.text: were randomly assigned to groups of four
with each group composed of two low-status members
who had just joined their college fraternity group
older students who had been active in the fraternity for at least two years

difficult_points: were randomly assigned 是被动语态，表示学生被随机分配到小组。
with each group composed of... 是 with 复合结构，补充每组构成。
who had just joined... 修饰 two low-status members，解释其低地位来源。
older students 是 two high-status members 的同位说明。

sentences.sentence: Laughter was recorded as each student took a turn at being teased by the others, involving the use of mildly insulting nicknames.
sentences.translation: 当每名学生轮流被其他人取笑时，研究人员记录了他们的笑声，这一过程涉及使用带有轻微冒犯意味的绰号。

structure.text: as each student took a turn at being teased by the others
involving the use of mildly insulting nicknames

difficult_points: was recorded 是被动语态，说明笑声被记录下来。
as 引导时间状语从句，表示记录发生在学生被取笑时。
took a turn at 后接动名词 being teased，表示“轮流被……”。
involving... 是现在分词短语，补充 teasing 的具体方式。

sentences.sentence: Analysis revealed that, as expected, high-status individuals produced more dominant laughs and fewer submissive laughs relative to the low-status individuals.
sentences.translation: 分析显示，正如预期的那样，与低地位个体相比，高地位个体发出了更多支配性笑声和更少服从性笑声。

structure.text: that, as expected, high-status individuals produced more dominant laughs and fewer submissive laughs relative to the low-status individuals

difficult_points: revealed 后接 that 宾语从句，说明分析得出的发现。
as expected 是插入语，表示结果符合研究预期。
relative to 表示比较基准，强调高地位者相对于低地位者的差异。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, low-status individuals were more likely to change their laughter based on their position of power; that is, the newcomers produced more dominant laughs when they were in the 'powerful' role of teasers.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，低地位个体更可能根据自己的权力位置改变笑声；也就是说，当这些新成员处在取笑者这一“有权势”的角色中时，他们会发出更多支配性笑声。

structure.text: were more likely to change their laughter
based on their position of power
that is, the newcomers produced more dominant laughs
when they were in the 'powerful' role of teasers

difficult_points: were more likely to 表示概率更高，不是绝对都会这样做。
based on... 是过去分词短语，说明改变笑声的依据。
that is 引出对前半句的解释说明。
when 引导时间/情境状语从句，说明新成员何时发出更多支配性笑声。

sentences.sentence: Dominant laughter was higher in pitch, louder, and more variable in tone than submissive laughter.
sentences.translation: 支配性笑声比服从性笑声音调更高、音量更大，语调也更加多变。

structure.text: was higher in pitch, louder, and more variable in tone
higher in pitch, louder, and more variable in tone

difficult_points: 三个比较级 higher、louder、more variable 并列描述 dominant laughter。
than submissive laughter 引出比较对象。
in pitch 和 in tone 分别限定音高和音调方面的差异。

sentences.sentence: A random group of volunteers then listened to an equal number of dominant and submissive laughs from both the high- and low-status individuals, and were asked to estimate the social status of the laugher.
sentences.translation: 随后，一组随机选出的志愿者听取了来自高地位和低地位个体数量相等的支配性与服从性笑声，并被要求估计发笑者的社会地位。

structure.text: then listened to an equal number of dominant and submissive laughs
from both the high- and low-status individuals
and were asked to estimate the social status of the laugher
to estimate the social status of the laugher

difficult_points: then 继续标记实验的下一步。
an equal number of 表示两类笑声数量相等，用于控制变量。
were asked to 与前面的 listened to 共用主语 volunteers，但后者是被动结构。

sentences.sentence: In line with predictions, laughers producing dominant laughs were perceived to be significantly higher in status than laughers producing submissive laughs.
sentences.translation: 与预测一致，发出支配性笑声的人被认为比发出服从性笑声的人社会地位显著更高。

structure.text: were perceived to be significantly higher in status

difficult_points: In line with predictions 表示结果符合预测。
producing dominant laughs 和 producing submissive laughs 分别后置修饰两类 laughers。
were perceived to be 是被动结构，强调志愿者的感知判断。
than 引出比较对象，比较的是被认为的社会地位高低。

sentences.sentence: 'This was particularly true for low-status individuals, who were rated as significantly higher in status when displaying a dominant versus submissive laugh,' Oveis and colleagues note.
sentences.translation: 奥维斯及其同事指出：“这一点对低地位个体尤其如此；当他们表现出支配性笑声而非服从性笑声时，其地位评分显著更高。”

structure.text: This was particularly true for low-status individuals
who were rated as significantly higher in status
when displaying a dominant versus submissive laugh

difficult_points: This 指上一句中支配性笑声会让人被感知为地位更高这一发现。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 low-status individuals 的评分变化。
when displaying... 是省略结构，相当于 when they were displaying。
dominant versus submissive 表示两种笑声之间的对比。

sentences.sentence: 'Thus, by strategically displaying more dominant laughter when the context allows, low-status individuals may achieve higher status in the eyes of others.'
sentences.translation: “因此，通过在情境允许时有策略地表现出更多支配性笑声，低地位个体可能会在他人眼中获得更高地位。”

structure.text: by strategically displaying more dominant laughter

difficult_points: Thus 表示根据前面实验结果得出的推论。
by strategically displaying... 表示实现 higher status 的方式。
when the context allows 限定这一策略并非任何场合都适用。
may achieve 使用情态动词，表示可能性而非必然结果。

sentences.sentence: However, high-status individuals were rated as high-status whether they produced their natural dominant laugh or tried to do a submissive one.
sentences.translation: 然而，高地位个体无论是发出自然的支配性笑声，还是试图发出服从性笑声，都会被评为高地位。

structure.text: whether they produced their natural dominant laugh or tried to do a submissive one
tried to do a submissive one

difficult_points: However 表示与低地位者的策略性变化形成转折。
were rated as high-status 是被动结构，表示别人对他们的评价。
whether...or... 引导让步/条件关系，表示两种情况评价都不变。
one 指代 laugh，避免重复 submissive laugh。

sentences.sentence: Another study, conducted by David Cheng and Lu Wang of Australian National University, was based on the hypothesis that humour might provide a respite from tedious situations in the workplace.
sentences.translation: 另一项由澳大利亚国立大学的大卫·程和王璐开展的研究，是基于这样一个假设：幽默可能会让人从工作场所的乏味情境中得到片刻喘息。

structure.text: conducted by David Cheng and Lu Wang of Australian National University
that humour might provide a respite from tedious situations in the workplace
from tedious situations in the workplace

difficult_points: conducted by... 是过去分词短语，插入说明研究者。
was based on 表示研究建立在某一假设基础上。
that 引导同位语从句，解释 hypothesis 的具体内容。
might provide 表示假设中的可能性，而不是已经证实的事实。

sentences.sentence: This 'mental break' might facilitate the replenishment of mental resources.
sentences.translation: 这种“心理休息”可能有助于补充心理资源。
difficult_points: This 'mental break' 指上一句中的幽默带来的 respite。
might 表示研究假设中的可能性。
the replenishment of mental resources 是名词化表达，意为心理资源的补充。

sentences.sentence: To test this theory, the researchers recruited 74 business students, ostensibly for an experiment on perception.
sentences.translation: 为了检验这一理论，研究人员招募了74名商科学生，表面上是为了进行一项关于感知的实验。

structure.text: ostensibly for an experiment on perception

difficult_points: To test this theory 是目的状语，说明招募学生的研究目的。
ostensibly 表示“表面上”，暗示真实实验目的可能并非如此。
for an experiment on perception 补充研究对参与者宣称的实验主题。

sentences.sentence: First, the students performed a tedious task in which they had to cross out every instance of the letter 'e' over two pages of text.
sentences.translation: 首先，学生们完成了一项乏味的任务：他们必须在两页文本中划掉每一个字母“e”。

structure.text: in which they had to cross out every instance of the letter 'e' over two pages of text
cross out every instance of the letter 'e'

difficult_points: First 标记实验步骤的第一步。
in which 引导定语从句，修饰 a tedious task。
had to 表示任务要求而非自愿行为。
every instance of the letter 'e' 指字母 e 的每一次出现。

sentences.sentence: The students then were randomly assigned to watch a video clip eliciting either humour, contentment, or neutral feelings.
sentences.translation: 随后，学生们被随机分配去观看一个会引发幽默感、满足感或中性感受的视频片段。

structure.text: then were randomly assigned to watch a video clip
eliciting either humour, contentment, or neutral feelings
either humour, contentment, or neutral feelings

difficult_points: then 表示实验进入下一步骤。
were randomly assigned 是被动语态，强调分组由研究设计决定。
eliciting... 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 video clip。
either...or... 列出视频可能引发的三类情绪。

sentences.sentence: Some watched a clip of the BBC comedy Mr. Bean, others a relaxing scene with dolphins swimming in the ocean, and others a factual video about the management profession.
sentences.translation: 一些人观看了BBC喜剧《憨豆先生》的片段，另一些人观看海豚在海中游泳的放松场景，还有一些人观看关于管理职业的纪实视频。

structure.text: Some watched a clip of the BBC comedy Mr. Bean
others a relaxing scene with dolphins swimming in the ocean
and others a factual video about the management profession
with dolphins swimming in the ocean

difficult_points: others 后省略了 watched，形成并列省略结构。
Mr. Bean 中的句点是名称缩写的一部分，不能误当作句子结束。
with dolphins swimming in the ocean 是 with 复合结构，修饰 relaxing scene。
三个并列成分对应三种实验视频条件。

sentences.sentence: The students then completed a task requiring persistence in which they were asked to guess the potential performance of employees based on provided profiles, and were told that making 10 correct assessments in a row would lead to a win.
sentences.translation: 随后，学生们完成了一项需要坚持的任务：他们被要求根据给定资料猜测员工的潜在表现，并被告知连续作出10次正确评估就会获胜。

structure.text: then completed a task requiring persistence
in which they were asked to guess the potential performance of employees based on provided profiles
and were told that making 10 correct assessments in a row would lead to a win
making 10 correct assessments in a row would lead to a win

difficult_points: requiring persistence 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 task。
in which 引导定语从句，说明任务的具体内容。
were asked 和 were told 是并列被动谓语，描述学生接到的两个要求/说明。
making 10 correct assessments in a row 是 that 从句中的主语。

sentences.sentence: However, the software was programmed such that it was nearly impossible to achieve 10 consecutive correct answers.
sentences.translation: 然而，软件被设置成几乎不可能连续答对10题。

structure.text: such that it was nearly impossible to achieve 10 consecutive correct answers

difficult_points: However 表明真实任务难度与学生被告知的获胜条件形成转折。
was programmed 是被动语态，说明软件经过人为设定。
such that 引出结果，说明程序设置导致的结果。
it 是形式主语，真正的动作是 to achieve 10 consecutive correct answers。

sentences.sentence: Participants were allowed to quit the task at any point.
sentences.translation: 参与者可以在任何时候退出这项任务。

structure.text: were allowed to quit the task

difficult_points: were allowed to 是被动结构，表示实验规则允许参与者退出。
to quit the task 说明被允许的具体行为。
at any point 强调退出时间没有限制。

sentences.sentence: Students who had watched the Mr. Bean video ended up spending significantly more time working on the task, making twice as many predictions as the other two groups.
sentences.translation: 观看过《憨豆先生》视频的学生最终花了显著更多时间做这项任务，作出的预测数量是另外两组的两倍。

structure.text: Students who had watched the Mr. Bean video
who had watched the Mr. Bean video
ended up spending significantly more time working on the task
making twice as many predictions as the other two groups

difficult_points: who had watched... 是定语从句，限定 Students 指幽默视频组。
ended up spending 表示最终结果，与研究预期相关。
significantly more 是统计意义上的显著更多。
twice as many...as... 表示数量是比较对象的两倍。

sentences.sentence: Cheng and Wang then replicated these results in a second study, during which they had participants complete long multiplication questions by hand.
sentences.translation: 程和王随后在第二项研究中复制了这些结果，在这项研究中他们让参与者手算长乘法题。

structure.text: during which they had participants complete long multiplication questions by hand
had participants complete long multiplication questions

difficult_points: replicated these results 表示第二项研究重复并验证了前一研究结果。
during which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 a second study。
had participants complete 是使役结构，表示让参与者完成任务。
by hand 表示手工计算，强调任务的乏味和耗费心力。

sentences.sentence: Again, participants who watched the humorous video spent significantly more time working on this tedious task and completed more questions correctly than did the students in either of the other groups.
sentences.translation: 同样，观看幽默视频的参与者在这项乏味任务上花费的时间显著更长，正确完成的问题也多于另外任一组学生。

structure.text: participants who watched the humorous video
spent significantly more time working on this tedious task
than did the students in either of the other groups

difficult_points: Again 表明第二项研究结果与第一项一致。
who watched the humorous video 限定 participants，指幽默视频组。
spent...and completed... 是并列谓语，说明两个表现指标。
than did the students... 使用比较从句中的倒装助动词 did。

sentences.sentence: 'Although humour has been found to help relieve stress and facilitate social relationships, the traditional view of task performance implies that individuals should avoid things such as humour that may distract them from the accomplishment of task goals,' Cheng and Wang conclude.
sentences.translation: 程和王总结道：“尽管人们已经发现幽默有助于缓解压力并促进社会关系，但关于任务表现的传统观点暗示，个人应避免幽默等可能使他们偏离任务目标实现的事物。”

structure.text: Although humour has been found to help relieve stress and facilitate social relationships
humour has been found to help relieve stress and facilitate social relationships
the traditional view of task performance
that individuals should avoid things such as humour that may distract them from the accomplishment of task goals
that may distract them from the accomplishment of task goals

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，先承认幽默已有的积极作用。
has been found to 是现在完成时被动结构，表示研究已经发现。
that individuals should avoid... 是 implies 的宾语从句。
that may distract them... 是定语从句，修饰 things such as humour。

sentences.sentence: 'We suggest that humour is not only enjoyable but more importantly, energising.'
sentences.translation: “我们认为，幽默不仅令人愉快，更重要的是，它能给人补充能量。”

structure.text: that humour is not only enjoyable but more importantly, energising
not only enjoyable but more importantly, energising

difficult_points: suggest 后接 that 宾语从句，表达作者的结论性观点。
not only...but... 连接两个表语，后者 energising 被强调为更重要。
more importantly 是插入语，强调幽默的激励作用超过单纯愉快。
